decision_commentary: Completeness 4: plan_set_components array has 8 components covering architectural, structural, MEP coordination, HVAC clinical, medical gas, fire protection, detail sheets, and COFEPRIS permit-ready package — this is strong. However the rubric specifies plan_set_components schema as requiring cross_sections, full_plan_book, technical_coordination fields specifically. The executive-plans.json uses a string array rather than the structured schema with those specific keys. Content is clearly present (detail sheets = cross_sections; 8-component set = full_plan_book; engineering_integration_confirmed=true = technical_coordination), but schema alignment is partial. Docking to 4. Accuracy 5: engineering_integration_confirmed=true, conflicts_resolved=true; all systems from engineering-package.json confirmed integrated including the 2 conditional specialized systems (hvac_clinical and medical_gas). client_signoff_milestone='M4 — Construction Documents Delivered' correctly named. Clarity 4: package is described well and references 'self-contained' in notes, but the plan_set_notes reference back to engineering-package.json (one external reference). State_sync 3 (simulated). Timing 5: executive plans complete before bidding (DG-10 timestamp 2026-11-20, Phase 5 Ofelia start per schedule 2026-11-20 — correct). Decision_readiness 5: Marcela can approve and hand off to Ofelia immediately. Auto-fail: no missing cross sections (detail sheets cover this) — PASS; conflicts_resolved=true — PASS; all required system drawings present — PASS.
celia_routing_notes: DG-10: decision=approve, route_to=Ofelia. All 11 payload fields present.
gaps: Schema uses string array for plan_set_components rather than structured object with cross_sections/full_plan_book/technical_coordination keys as rubric specifies
